title: Anathallis lobiserrata
intro: Anathallis lobiserrata, also known as the Lobed Anathallis, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Anathallis lobiserrata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Anathallis lobiserrata is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into topical ointments and creams to harness its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Anathallis lobiserrata belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristic orchid-like flowers and growth habits. Further, Anathallis lobiserrata is classified as a species within the genus Anathallis. The genus Anathallis is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a subgroup of the orchid family. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is characterized by its small to medium-sized flowers and often epiphytic growth.
